lede: A look at May 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bob Dylan and Joseph Brodsky, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Quetta train bombing
events.desc: A suicide bombing targeting a shuttle train in Quetta, Balochistan, killed at least 48 people and injured 98 others.

events.title: Uvalde school shooting
events.desc: A mass shooting occurs at Robb Elementary School in Uvalde, Texas, United States, resulting in the deaths of 21 people, including 19 children.

events.title: Section 28
events.desc: Section 28 of the United Kingdom's Local Government Act 1988, a controversial amendment stating that a local authority cannot intentionally promote homosexuality, is enacted.

events.title: Project Mercury
events.desc: Project Mercury: American astronaut Scott Carpenter orbits the Earth three times in the Aurora 7 space capsule.

events.title: 1948 Arab–Israeli War
events.desc: Arab–Israeli War: Egypt captures the Israeli kibbutz of Yad Mordechai, but the five-day effort gives Israeli forces time to prepare enough to stop the Egyptian advance a week later.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of the Atlantic: In the Battle of the Denmark Strait, the German battleship Bismarck sinks the pride of the Royal Navy, HMS Hood, killing all but three crewmen.

events.title: Amy Johnson
events.desc: Amy Johnson lands in Darwin, Northern Territory, becoming the first woman to fly solo from England to Australia (she left on May 5 for the 11,000 mile flight).

events.title: Brooklyn Bridge
events.desc: The Brooklyn Bridge in New York City is opened to traffic after 14 years of construction.

events.title: Irish Rebellion of 1798
events.desc: The Irish Rebellion of 1798 led by the United Irishmen against British rule begins.

events.title: Erik XIV
events.desc: Erik XIV of Sweden and his guards murder five incarcerated Swedish nobles.

births.name: Bob Dylan
births.line: American singer-songwriter, guitarist, artist, writer, and producer; Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Joseph Brodsky
births.line: Russian-American poet and essayist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Terry Callier
births.line: American soul, folk and jazz guitarist and singer-songwriter

births.name: Mikhail Sholokhov
births.line: Russian novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Annette von Droste-Hülshoff, German author and composer
Georg Ernst Stahl, German physician and chemist

observances.text: Bermuda Day (Bermuda), celebrated on the nearest weekday if May 24 falls on the weekend.
Christian feast day: Anna Pak Agi (one of The Korean Martyrs)
